english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Philippians

verses.latin_vulgate: Si qua ergo consolatio in Christo, si quod solatium caritatis, si qua societas spiritus, si qua viscera miserationis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τις οὖν παράκλησις ἐν χριστῷ εἴ τι παραμύθιον ἀγάπης εἴ τις κοινωνία πνεύματος εἴ τινα σπλάγχνα καὶ οἰκτιρμοί
verses.king_james: If *there be* therefore any consolation in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any bowels and mercies,

verses.latin_vulgate: implete gaudium meum ut idem sapiatis, eamdem caritatem habentes, unanimes, idipsum sentientes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πληρώσατέ μου τὴν χαρὰν ἵνα τὸ αὐτὸ φρονῆτε τὴν αὐτὴν ἀγάπην ἔχοντες σύμψυχοι τὸ ἓν φρονοῦντες
verses.king_james: Fulfil ye my joy, that ye be likeminded, having the same love, *being* of one accord, of one mind.

verses.latin_vulgate: nihil per contentionem, neque per inanem gloriam: sed in humilitate superiores sibi invicem arbitrantes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδὲν κατὰ ἐριθείαν ἢ κενοδοξίαν ἀλλὰ τῇ ταπεινοφροσύνῃ ἀλλήλους ἡγούμενοι ὑπερέχοντας ἑαυτῶν
verses.king_james: *Let* nothing *be done* through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: non quæ sua sunt singuli considerantes, sed ea quæ aliorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ τὰ ἑαυτῶν ἕκαστος σκοπεῖτε, ἀλλὰ καὶ τὰ ἑτέρων ἕκαστος
verses.king_james: Look not every man on his own things, but every man also on the things of others.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc enim sentite in vobis, quod et in Christo Jesu:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο γὰρ φρονείσθω ἐν ὑμῖν ὃ καὶ ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui cum in forma Dei esset, non rapinam arbitratus est esse se æqualem Deo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς ἐν μορφῇ θεοῦ ὑπάρχων οὐχ ἁρπαγμὸν ἡγήσατο τὸ εἶναι ἴσα θεῷ
verses.king_james: Who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed semetipsum exinanivit, formam servi accipiens, in similitudinem hominum factus, et habitu inventus ut homo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἑαυτὸν ἐκένωσεν μορφὴν δούλου λαβών ἐν ὁμοιώματι ἀνθρώπων γενόμενος
verses.king_james: But made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a servant, and was made in the likeness of men:

verses.latin_vulgate: Humiliavit semetipsum factus obediens usque ad mortem, mortem autem crucis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καί σχήματι εὑρεθείς ὥς ἄνθρωπος ἐταπείνωσεν ἑαυτὸν γενόμενος ὑπήκοος μέχρι θανάτου θανάτου δὲ σταυροῦ
verses.king_james: And being found in fashion as a man, he humbled himself, and became obedient unto death, even the death of the cross.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod et Deus exaltavit illum, et donavit illi nomen, quod est super omne nomen:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ καὶ ὁ θεὸς αὐτὸν ὑπερύψωσεν καὶ ἐχαρίσατο αὐτῷ ὄνομα τὸ ὑπὲρ πᾶν ὄνομα
verses.king_james: Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above every name:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut in nomine Jesu omne genu flectatur cælestium, terrestrium et infernorum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι ἰησοῦ πᾶν γόνυ κάμψῃ ἐπουρανίων καὶ ἐπιγείων καὶ καταχθονίων
verses.king_james: That at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of *things* in heaven, and *things* in earth, and *things* under the earth;

verses.latin_vulgate: et omnis lingua confiteatur, quia Dominus Jesus Christus in gloria est Dei Patris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶσα γλῶσσα ἐξομολογήσηται ὅτι κύριος ἰησοῦς χριστὸς εἰς δόξαν θεοῦ πατρός
verses.king_james: And *that* every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ *is* Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque carissimi mei (sicut semper obedistis), non ut in præsentia mei tantum, sed multo magis nunc in absentia mea, cum metu et tremore vestram salutem operamini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε ἀγαπητοί μου καθὼς πάντοτε ὑπηκούσατε μὴ ὡς ἐν τῇ παρουσίᾳ μου μόνον ἀλλὰ νῦν πολλῷ μᾶλλον ἐν τῇ ἀπουσίᾳ μου μετὰ φόβου καὶ τρόμου τὴν ἑαυτῶν σωτηρίαν κατεργάζεσθε
verses.king_james: Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus est enim, qui operatur in vobis et velle, et perficere pro bona voluntate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ θεὸς γάρ ἐστιν ὁ ἐνεργῶν ἐν ὑμῖν καὶ τὸ θέλειν καὶ τὸ ἐνεργεῖν ὑπὲρ τῆς εὐδοκίας
verses.king_james: For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of *his* good pleasure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia autem facite sine murmurationibus et hæsitationibus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα ποιεῖτε χωρὶς γογγυσμῶν καὶ διαλογισμῶν
verses.king_james: Do all things without murmurings and disputings:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut sitis sine querela, et simplices filii Dei, sine reprehensione in medio nationis pravæ et perversæ: inter quos lucetis sicut luminaria in mundo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα γένησθε ἄμεμπτοι καὶ ἀκέραιοι τέκνα θεοῦ ἀμώμητα ἐν μέσῳ γενεᾶς σκολιᾶς καὶ διεστραμμένης ἐν οἷς φαίνεσθε ὡς φωστῆρες ἐν κόσμῳ
verses.king_james: That ye may be blameless and harmless, the sons of God, without rebuke, in the midst of a crooked and perverse nation, among whom ye shine as lights in the world;

verses.latin_vulgate: verbum vitæ continentes ad gloriam meam in die Christi, quia non in vacuum cucurri, neque in vacuum laboravi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λόγον ζωῆς ἐπέχοντες εἰς καύχημα ἐμοὶ εἰς ἡμέραν χριστοῦ ὅτι οὐκ εἰς κενὸν ἔδραμον οὐδὲ εἰς κενὸν ἐκοπίασα
verses.king_james: Holding forth the word of life; that I may rejoice in the day of Christ, that I have not run in vain, neither laboured in vain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et si immolor supra sacrificium, et obsequium fidei vestræ, gaudeo, et congratulor omnibus vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ εἰ καὶ σπένδομαι ἐπὶ τῇ θυσίᾳ καὶ λειτουργίᾳ τῆς πίστεως ὑμῶν χαίρω καὶ συγχαίρω πᾶσιν ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Yea, and if I be offered upon the sacrifice and service of your faith, I joy, and rejoice with you all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Idipsum autem et vos gaudete, et congratulamini mihi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δ᾽ αὐτὸ καὶ ὑμεῖς χαίρετε καὶ συγχαίρετέ μοι
verses.king_james: For the same cause also do ye joy, and rejoice with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Spero autem in Domino Jesu, Timotheum me cito mittere ad vos: ut et ego bono animo sim, cognitis quæ circa vos sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλπίζω δὲ ἐν κυρίῳ ἰησοῦ τιμόθεον ταχέως πέμψαι ὑμῖν ἵνα κἀγὼ εὐψυχῶ γνοὺς τὰ περὶ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timotheus shortly unto you, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know your state.

verses.latin_vulgate: Neminem enim habeo tam unanimem, qui sincera affectione pro vobis sollicitus sit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδένα γὰρ ἔχω ἰσόψυχον ὅστις γνησίως τὰ περὶ ὑμῶν μεριμνήσει
verses.king_james: For I have no man likeminded, who will naturally care for your state.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes enim quæ sua sunt quærunt, non quæ sunt Jesu Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ πάντες γὰρ τὰ ἑαυτῶν ζητοῦσιν οὐ τὰ τοῦ χριστοῦ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: For all seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ's.

verses.latin_vulgate: Experimentum autem ejus cognoscite, quia sicut patri filius, mecum servivit in Evangelio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὴν δὲ δοκιμὴν αὐτοῦ γινώσκετε ὅτι ὡς πατρὶ τέκνον σὺν ἐμοὶ ἐδούλευσεν εἰς τὸ εὐαγγέλιον
verses.king_james: But ye know the proof of him, that, as a son with the father, he hath served with me in the gospel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hunc igitur spero me mittere ad vos, mox ut videro quæ circa me sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτον μὲν οὖν ἐλπίζω πέμψαι ὡς ἂν ἀπίδω τὰ περὶ ἐμὲ ἐξαυτῆς
verses.king_james: Him therefore I hope to send presently, so soon as I shall see how it will go with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confido autem in Domino quoniam et ipse veniam ad vos cito.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πέποιθα δὲ ἐν κυρίῳ ὅτι καὶ αὐτὸς ταχέως ἐλεύσομαι
verses.king_james: But I trust in the Lord that I also myself shall come shortly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Necessarium autem existimavi Epaphroditum fratrem, et cooperatorem, et commilitonem meum, vestrum autem apostolum, et ministrum necessitatis meæ, mittere ad vos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀναγκαῖον δὲ ἡγησάμην ἐπαφρόδιτον τὸν ἀδελφὸν καὶ συνεργὸν καὶ συστρατιώτην μου ὑμῶν δὲ ἀπόστολον καὶ λειτουργὸν τῆς χρείας μου πέμψαι πρὸς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: Yet I supposed it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, and companion in labour, and fellowsoldier, but your messenger, and he that ministered to my wants.

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam quidem omnes vos desiderabat: et mœstus erat, propterea quod audieratis illum infirmatum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπειδὴ ἐπιποθῶν ἦν πάντας ὑμᾶς καὶ ἀδημονῶν διότι ἠκούσατε ὅτι ἠσθένησεν
verses.king_james: For he longed after you all, and was full of heaviness, because that ye had heard that he had been sick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam et infirmatus est usque ad mortem: sed Deus misertus est ejus: non solum autem ejus, verum etiam et mei, ne tristitiam super tristitiam haberem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γὰρ ἠσθένησεν παραπλήσιον θανάτῳ ἀλλ᾽ ὁ θεὸς αὐτόν ἠλέησεν οὐκ αὐτὸν δὲ μόνον ἀλλὰ καὶ ἐμέ ἵνα μὴ λύπην ἐπὶ λύπῃ σχῶ
verses.king_james: For indeed he was sick nigh unto death: but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Festinantius ergo misi illum, ut viso eo iterum gaudeatis, et ego sine tristitia sim.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σπουδαιοτέρως οὖν ἔπεμψα αὐτὸν ἵνα ἰδόντες αὐτὸν πάλιν χαρῆτε κἀγὼ ἀλυπότερος ὦ
verses.king_james: I sent him therefore the more carefully, that, when ye see him again, ye may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.

verses.latin_vulgate: Excipite itaque illum cum omni gaudio in Domino, et ejusmodi cum honore habetote;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσδέχεσθε οὖν αὐτὸν ἐν κυρίῳ μετὰ πάσης χαρᾶς καὶ τοὺς τοιούτους ἐντίμους ἔχετε
verses.king_james: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all gladness; and hold such in reputation:

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam propter opus Christi usque ad mortem accessit, tradens animam suam ut impleret id quod ex vobis deerat erga meum obsequium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι διὰ τὸ ἔργον τοῦ χριστοῦ μέχρι θανάτου ἤγγισεν παραβουλευσάμενος τῇ ψυχῇ ἵνα ἀναπληρώσῃ τὸ ὑμῶν ὑστέρημα τῆς πρός με λειτουργίας
verses.king_james: Because for the work of Christ he was nigh unto death, not regarding his life, to supply your lack of service toward me.
